div1.entryFree:
Rap, 1) to strike with a quick blow, to knock: knock me at this gate and r. me well, Shr. I, 2, 12. Shr. I, 2, 12 she --ed'em o' the coxcombs with a stick, Lr. II, 4, 125 (Ff knapped).
2) to affect with ecstasy, to transport: what, dear sir, thus --s you? Cymb. I, 6, 51. Partic. rapt == a) enraptured, transported: more dances my r. heart than when I first my wedded mistress saw bestride my threshold, Cor. IV, 5, 122. of royal hope that he seems r. withal, Mcb. I, 3, 57. how our partner's r. Mcb. I, 3, 57 whiles I stood r. in the wonder of it I, 5, 6. b) beside one's self: I am r. and cannot cover the monstrous bulk of this ingratitude with any size of words, Tim. V, 1, 67. c) quite absorbed, engrossed; with in: transported and r. in secret studies, Tp. I, 2, 77. I was much r. in this, Troil. III, 3, 123. you are r., sir, in some work, Tim. I, 1, 19. (cf. Enrapt).
